crime: For years she had maintained her world in full — the rooms, the hours, the companion's face sketched again and again. One drawing hung above her bunk.

For months she had shared a library table with a girl who talked while she listened. One evening she told the girl about her world. The girl watched her phone the whole time and at the end said: sorry — what?
The girl ate early and spent her evenings alone at the guest house, in the fire-sprite room, for the quiet.

That same evening, while she sat in plain sight at her place in the dining hall, she sent the companion out — through the entrance, up the path, into the fire-sprite room. It struck the girl with a stone from the path, and she let it go.
A girl coming up the path had passed a stranger leaving the guest house.
The girl was found beaten in the fire-sprite room before lock, the stone beside her. Every prisoner had been at dinner through that hour, each placed by the others — no one was missing.
The stranger appeared on no roll on the island. The face the girl on the path had seen was the one pinned above her bunk.
fields.epitaph: She drew the only eyes that ever stayed.
fields.epithet: She Who Built Her Own Witness
execution: A small furnished room stood whole on the execution stand, complete to its curtains. She was bound in the chair at its center.
Mechanical arms began at the furnishings — table, shelf, curtains — and took them away piece by piece.
The walls came away panel by panel, then the rafters, working inward.
The roof-beam was the last piece taken. It came down onto the chair where she sat.

magic.name: the Companion
magic.text: The Companion — She can set the person she has shaped in her mind walking in the world, real to every sense, doing her will even beyond her sight; when she lets it go, nothing of it remains.
